course_description:
This course is part of a suite of Literacy and English courses in the Master of Teaching (Primary) degree. Three interrelated aspects of the teaching profession provide a developmental framework for the courses: knowing yourself as a Literacy/English teacher; knowing your students; and knowing the disciplinary content of the subject English as well as the place of literacy teaching and learning within other curriculum areas. In this course, students will develop a repertoire of teaching strategies and practices related to the teaching of writing and children’s literature; identify a range of assessment techniques and strategies suitable for diverse contexts; and build their knowledge of key concepts related to literacy and the discipline of English.

For preservice teachers enrolled in the ‘standard’ Master of Teaching (Primary) Program , this course provides an introduction to school life through a 5-day (one week) block of professional experience and these preservice teachers are required to have a valid Blue Card for the Blue Card for the duration of the placement.

For preservice teachers enrolled in the ‘Turn to Teaching’ Internship Program , a professional experience placement is not part of this course .

EDUC7550 provides a comprehensive introduction to key aspects of the English learning area in the primary years of schooling. In the course, you will develop a repertoire of practices and strategies related to the teaching of writing and children's literature. You will identify and use a range of assessment techniques suitable for diverse contexts and build knowledge of key concepts related to the discipline of English. With the use of the Australian Curriculum: English, you will plan high-quality, intellectually rigorous writing activities and explore contemporary approaches for assessing student writing. Your completion of this course will be informed by key frameworks and approaches including the four resources model, the writing rope, and genre-based pedagogy. Strategies and resources utilised throughout EDUC7550, including embedded Information Communication Technologies (ICTs), are designed to enable you to demonstrate attainment of the Australian Professional Standards for Teachers (APST) at the graduate level. Specifically, the course's teaching and learning activities and assessment tasks target the following standards: 1.3, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.5, 3.1, 3.3, 3.4, 3.5, 4.1, 5.1, 5.2, 5.3, 5.4, 6.2, andﾠ6.4.

Additional timetable information
EDUC7550 is taught and assessed at Masters level. It is offered in a two-hour lecture and one-hour tutorial format.
course_aims: EDUC7550ﾠaims to build your knowledge and skills in planning for, teaching, and assessingﾠwritingﾠand children's literature experiencesﾠin primary school English contexts.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate research-informed knowledge, understanding and analysis of literacy teaching and assessment strategies and their application in teaching the primary school English Curriculum in ways that enhance inclusive participation of all students including those from diverse backgrounds (linguistic, cultural, religious and socioeconomic). (APST: 1.3, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.5, 3.1, 3.3, 5.1)

Identify teaching strategies that include a range of verbal and non-verbal communication strategies and resources including ICTs that support inclusive student participation and engagement in English Curriculum primary classroom activities and learning. (APST: 3.3, 3.4, 3.5, 4.1)

Demonstrate the capacity to effectively engage in assessment moderation and interpret student assessment data to evaluate student learning, provide timely and appropriate student feedback, and modify teaching practices to improve student learning. (APST: 5.2, 5.3, 5.4)

Demonstrate an understanding of the relevant and appropriate sources of professional learning for primary school English teachers and provide justification for continued professional learning in light of implications for improved student learning and teaching in the English Curriculum Learning Area. (APST: 6.2, 6.4)
Demonstrate clear, fluent, and coherent communication skills consistent with personal, professional, and academic conventions.

assessment_summary.category: Creative Production/ Exhibition, Essay/ Critique, Presentation
assessment_summary.title: 4 Quality literature book trailer and rationale

learning_activities.topic: Introduction Language, literacy, and culture; Introduction to the Australian Curriculum: English Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Contemporary literacy theories Four resources model; Writing rope; SFL/Genre pedagogy; Multiliteracies; Critical literacy Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Writing 1 Overview of writing; Grammar 1 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
Writing 2 Grammar 2 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Writing 3 Written genres; Structural features (stages, phases) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04
Writing 4 Teaching writing; Assessing writing Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Writing 5 Paragraphing; Punctuation; Handwriting and keyboarding Learning outcomes: L01, L03
Children's literature 1 Overview of children's literature; Ideological meanings; Semiotics Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Children's literature 2 Features of literary texts Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Children's literature 3 Multimodality; Multicultural children's literature Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
Children's literature 4 Genres of children's literature; Poetry Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Student presentations Sharing book trailers and oral presentations Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04
Conclusion and review Reflection on course content Learning outcomes: L03, L04

Professional Experience (Standard MTeach Students ONLY) Students part of the 'Standard' cohort will attend professional experience in a school. NOTE: Students part of the Turn to Teach Internship Program will NOT be required to attend this placement as part of their program.

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life.
